title: 10 best herbal teas for chapped lips
intro_desc: Herbal teas for chapped lips are infusions made from natural ingredients that help soothe and moisturize dry, cracked lips. These teas typically contain herbs and flowers known for their healing properties, such as chamomile, calendula, and rose petals. The antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds found in these herbs can help repair damaged lip tissue, reduce redness and irritation, and provide long-lasting hydration. By sipping on these soothing teas, individuals can experience relief from chapped lips, leading to improved comfort and confidence in their daily lives. For example, someone who suffers from dry, cracked lips due to cold weather or frequent lip-licking can benefit from regularly drinking herbal teas for chapped lips, ultimately enjoying smoother, healthier lips.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow teas helps with chapped lips because of its unique ability to provide soothing, calming, and healing properties. The antioxidants present in yarrow help to reduce inflammation and redness associated with chapped lips, while its astringent properties help to dry out excess moisture and promote the growth of healthy new tissue. Additionally, yarrow's antimicrobial properties help to prevent the spread of infection and promote quick recovery. As a result, sipping on herbal yarrow tea can provide instant relief from dry, cracked, or painful lips.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Yarrow tea has been shown to possess antimicrobial properties that can help combat fungal and bacterial infections that can contribute to chapped lips, promoting healing and soothing irritated skin.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and terpenes present in yarrow tea have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and redness associated with chapped lips, providing relief from discomfort and pain.

Wound-healing compounds: Yarrow tea contains saponins, which are known to promote wound healing by stimulating collagen synthesis, improving tissue repair, and enhancing the body's natural defense against infection.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing and protecting chapped lips.
Seeds: Containing fatty acids and essential oils, they help to moisturize and nourish dry, cracked lips.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh yarrow leaves and flowers in late morning or early afternoon when they are at peak potency.
Rinse the yarrow material with clean water to remove any dirt or debris from the fields or storage.
Combine one teaspoon of dried yarrow leaves and flowers or two teaspoons of fresh yarrow leaves and flowers with 8 ounces of boiling water in a teapot.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid using a tea infuser or a piece of cheesecloth into a cup.
Enjoy the tea hot or cold, sweetened or unsweetened, as desired, within 24 hours of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow teas helps with chapped lips because its soothing properties can effectively moisturize and soften dry, cracked skin. The mucilage present in marshmallows, which is extracted into a tea, forms a protective barrier on the skin's surface, locking in moisture and reducing irritation. This natural remedy also contains anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce redness and discomfort associated with chapped lips, providing long-lasting relief and comfort.
remedy_properties: Mucilage: Marshmallow tea contains a thick, gel-like substance called mucilage, which helps to soothe and protect dry, chapped lips by forming a protective barrier that locks in moisture.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: The antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds present in marshmallow tea, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help to reduce inflammation and swelling associated with chapped lips, promoting healing and comfort.
Emollients: Marshmallow tea is rich in emollients like vitamin E and saponins, which moisturize and soften the lips, reducing flakiness and discomfort while also providing long-lasting hydration.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilages, which help to soothe and protect dry, cracked lips by forming a protective barrier on their surface.
Bark: Containing a high concentration of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which can help reduce inflammation and promote healing of chapped lips.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh althaea officinalis leaves and stems from your garden or a trusted source measuring one cup of plant material for every 8 ounces water.
Rinse the harvested plant material with clean water to remove any dirt or debris then gently pat dry with a paper towel.
Combine the cleaned plant material with boiling water in a medium-sized pot and reduce heat to low simmering for 10-15 minutes allowing flavors to meld.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a large glass pitcher discarding solids and reserving liquid for consumption.
Allow the tea to steep for an additional 30 minutes to one hour in the refrigerator before serving chilled with honey sweetening to taste.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold teas helps with chapped lips because it is rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm irritated skin. The tea's flavonoids and polyphenols work to repair and protect the delicate lip tissue, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with chapping. Additionally, the tea's natural moisturizing properties help to hydrate and soften dry, cracked lips, leaving them feeling smooth and supple.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold tea has anti-inflammatory compounds like luteolin, which helps to reduce swelling and redness associated with chapped lips, providing relief from discomfort.

Antioxidant: The antioxidants present in pot marigold tea, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help to protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy lip tissue and preventing further dryness.
Emollient: Pot marigold contains mucilages, which are natural emollients that help to lock in moisture and soothe dry, chapped lips, providing instant relief and comfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants, roots help soothe and calm irritated skin while providing hydration for dry, chapped lips.
Seeds: With anti-inflammatory properties, seeds reduce redness and swelling associated with chapped lips, promoting healing and comfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 tablespoons of fresh pot marigold flowers or 1 teaspoon dried pot marigold petals.
Measure 1 cup of boiling water and pour it over the pot marigold mixture in a heat-resistant teapot.
Steep for 10-15 minutes to allow the flavors and nutrients to infuse into the tea.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids and retain the liquid.
Serve the tea hot, sweetened with honey if desired, and enjoy within 24 hours of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Roman chamomile teas helps with chapped lips because of its soothing and calming properties. The apigenin in chamomile binds to skin receptors, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with dry, cracked lips. Additionally, the tea's antioxidants and flavonoids help to hydrate and regenerate the lip tissue, leaving it soft, smooth, and moisturized. By drinking this tea regularly, you can alleviate chapped lip symptoms and enjoy a comfortable, healthy smile.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Roman Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with chapped lips.
Soilant and moisturizing properties: The tea's soothing agents, such as apigenin, calm dry, irritated skin and provide long-lasting hydration to chapped lips.
Antioxidant properties: Roman Chamomile's antioxidants protect the lips from environmental stressors, free radicals, and damage caused by UV rays, promoting overall lip health.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Roman chamomile plant contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm chapped lips.
Flowers: The flowers of the Roman chamomile plant have anti-irritant properties that reduce redness and inflammation, providing relief from chapped lips.
remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried roman chamomile flowers per cup of boiling water and measure accurately.
Bring the measured water to a boil in a tea kettle or pot and let it simmer for 3-5 minutes.
Remove the kettle from the heat and add the dried roman chamomile flowers, allowing them steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or tea infuser, discarding the solids.
Serve the herbal tea hot or let it cool to room temperature, enjoying within 30 minutes of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eastern purple coneflower teas helps with chapped lips because of its unique properties. The tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe dry, chapped skin and reduce redness and irritation. Additionally, the antioxidants present in the tea help to hydrate and moisturize the lips, providing long-lasting relief from chapping and dryness. As a result, drinking eastern purple coneflower tea can provide a natural and effective remedy for chapped lips, promoting healthy and comfortable lip care.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: The tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like kaempferol and quercetin, which can help reduce inflammation and soothe dry, chapped lips.
Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in the tea, such as vitamin E and polyphenols, work to protect the lips from damage caused by environmental stressors and promote healing of cracked skin.

Wound-healing properties: Eastern Purple Coneflower contains saponins, which have been shown to stimulate collagen production and improve wound healing, helping to repair damaged lip tissue and reduce the appearance of cracks and fissures.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm dry, chapped lips.
Bark: The bark has astringent properties that help to tighten and protect dry skin on the lips.
remedy_recipe: Gather one tablespoon of dried echinacea purpurea flowers or two tablespoons of fresh flowers.
Combine the gathered echinacea with 8 ounces of boiling water in a medium-sized teapot or infuser.
Allow the mixture to steep for 10-15 minutes depending on desired strength and flavor.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard solids.
Add one teaspoon of honey or lemon juice to taste and serve immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel teas helps with chapped lips because of its soothing and moisturizing properties. The tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as anethole and flavonoids, work to calm irritated skin and reduce redness. Additionally, the tea's natural oils help to lock in moisture, leaving lips feeling soft and supple. As you sip the tea, the warm liquid helps to rehydrate and nourish dry, chapped lips, providing long-lasting relief from discomfort and irritation.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Fennel tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and polyphenols, help reduce inflammation and soothe dry, irritated skin on the lips.

Antioxidant properties: Fennel tea's antioxidants combat free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and damage to lip tissue, promoting healthy cell growth and reducing the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles.

Soothing and protective mucilage properties: Fennel tea contains a thick, gel-like substance called mucilage, which forms a protective barrier on the skin, locking in moisture and protecting the lips from further irritation and dryness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots are anti-inflammatory and antiseptic, making them effective in soothing and calming irritated skin and lips.
Seeds: Fennel seeds are rich in antioxidants and have natural astringent properties that help to dry out and heal chapped lips.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh fennel leaves or use dried fennel flowers in the desired quantity of one tablespoon per cup.
Combine the harvested or dried fennel with boiling water in a teapot or infuser for steeping time of five to seven minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove any loose particles and solids.
Add honey or sugar to taste if desired and stir well before serving the herbal fennel tea warm or at room temperature.
Drink one to two cups of the fennel tea daily for relief from digestive issues such as bloating and cramps within thirty minutes to an hour after consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice teas helps with chapped lips because it provides a soothing and nourishing hydration to the dry, cracked skin. The glycyrrhizin in licorice root has anti-inflammatory properties that calm and comfort irritated lip tissue, while its flavonoids work to strengthen the skin's natural barrier function. As a result, regular consumption of licorice tea can help to lock in moisture and promote healthy, soft lips even in harsh weather conditions.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Licorice root contains anti-inflammatory compounds like glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, which help to reduce redness, swelling, and inflammation in the mouth and lips.

Soil-softening mucilage: Licorice root's mucilage (a thick, gel-like substance) helps to moisturize and protect chapped lips by creating a soothing barrier that traps moisture and shields them from harsh environmental factors.
Antioxidant activity: Licorice tea's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to lip dryness and irritation, promoting overall oral health and well-being.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that soothes and moisturizes dry, chapped lips.
Bark: The bark of the licorice plant has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce redness and swelling associated with chapped lips.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried licorice root for every 8 ounces of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes in a teapot or infuser.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Add honey to taste if desired and stir well before serving hot or cold.
Allow the tea to cool completely before refrigerating it for up to 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Witch hazel teas helps with chapped lips because of its astringent and anti-inflammatory properties. The tea's witch hazel extract tightens the skin on the lips, providing instant relief from dryness and cracking. Additionally, the antioxidants and tannins in the tea help to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and inflammation. As a result, witch hazel teas can provide long-lasting hydration and comfort to chapped lips, leaving them feeling soft, smooth, and supple.
remedy_properties: Astringent properties: Witch hazel tea's astringent properties help to dry out and soothe chapped lips by reducing moisture loss and inflammation, promoting a healthy balance of lip skin.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in witch hazel tea, such as gallic acid, help to reduce redness and swelling associated with chapped lips, providing relief from discomfort and pain.

Antimicrobial properties: Witch hazel tea's antimicrobial properties help to combat bacteria and other microorganisms that can contribute to chapped lip infections or irritations, promoting a healthy environment for healing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing anti-inflammatory compounds, roots help soothe and calm irritated lips.
Bark: Rich in tannins, bark provides astringent properties that can dry out and protect chapped lips.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried witch hazel leaves from the hamamelis virginiana plant.
Combine 1 tablespoon of dried witch hazel leaves with 8 ounces of boiling water in a teapot.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes, allowing the herbs to infuse and release their active compounds.
Strain the tea through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a large cup, discarding the solids.
Enjoy your herbal witch hazel tea warm, sweetened with honey if desired, within the next 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's wort teas helps with chapped lips because it contains hypericin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. When consumed as a tea, the active ingredients in St John's Wort are absorbed into the bloodstream and delivered to the skin, where they can help to soothe and calm dry, chapped lips. The tea's natural antiseptic and antibacterial qualities also work to combat any infection or irritation that may be contributing to lip dryness, providing fast and effective relief from discomfort and pain.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: St. John's Wort tea is rich in antioxidants, which help to neutralize free radicals and reduce inflammation caused by dry, chapped lips.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids present in St. John's Wort tea have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to soothe and calm irritated lips, reducing redness and swelling.

Wound-healing properties: St. John's Wort has been traditionally used to promote wound healing, including lip wounds caused by chapping or cracking. Its extract contains hyperforin, a compound that accelerates the healing process by stimulating collagen synthesis and improving blood flow.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and flavonoids, which help soothe and calm dry, chapped lips.
Flowers: Containing hyperforin, a compound with anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce lip irritation and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Gather one tablespoon of dried st john's wort flowers and leaves or four teaspoons of fresh herbs per cup.
Combine the herb with 8-10 ounces of boiling water in a teapot or infuser for 5-7 minutes.
Steep the mixture until the liquid reaches your desired strength and flavor for 2-4 hours.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids for 1 minute.
Enjoy the tea hot or let it cool to room temperature within 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender teas helps with chapped lips because its soothing and calming properties help to reduce inflammation and redness. The tea's antioxidants also work to repair and nourish dry, cracked skin, promoting healthy lip cell turnover. Additionally, the tea's gentle anti-inflammatory effects can help to alleviate the discomfort and pain associated with chapped lips, providing relief and promoting a sense of calm.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Lavender tea's antioxidants help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to dryness and cracking on the lips.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender tea's anti-inflammatory compounds can help reduce redness and swelling associated with chapped lips, promoting healing and soothing discomfort.
Moisturizing properties: The essential oils present in lavender tea, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, have natural moisturizing effects that can help to hydrate and soften the lips.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in calcium and potassium, lavender roots help to soothe and calm dry, chapped lips.
Seeds: Containing anti-inflammatory compounds, lavender seeds can be used to create a tea that reduces redness and swelling associated with chapped lips.
remedy_recipe: Measure out two teaspoons of dried english lavender flowers into a clean glass teapot.
Heat one cup of filtered water to a rolling boil in a separate pot on the stovetop.
Pour the boiling water over the lavender flowers and let it steep for five to seven minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a large ceramic cup.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before serving, garnished with a sprig of fresh lavender if desired.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat chapped lips with herbal teas is to brew a strong tea using chamomile or calendula flowers. Allow the tea to cool, then gently apply it to your lips with a cotton swab. The anti-inflammatory properties of these herbs can help soothe and heal cracked lips. For added moisture, mix in a small amount of honey or coconut oil before applying. Repeat this treatment several times a day, and remember to also stay hydrated by drinking plenty of fluids.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to chapped lips that are treated with herbal teas include dry, irritated, or inflamed skin. Certain herbal teas have anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties that can soothe and heal skin. For example, chamomile tea can be used as a compress to reduce redness and irritation, while calendula tea can be applied topically to promote wound healing and hydration. Other herbal teas, such as lavender and rosehip, also have soothing and anti-inflammatory effects that can help alleviate symptoms of chapped lips and other skin ailments. However, it is important to note that individual responses to herbal remedies may vary, and it is always best to consult with a healthcare provider before using any new treatments.
